patterns_discovered.name: Repository Pattern
patterns_discovered.description: Combines multiple data sources (network state + outbox database) into single abstraction for UI consumption

patterns_discovered.name: Reactive Data Combination
patterns_discovered.description: Uses Flow.combine to merge two independent reactive streams into unified state emissions

patterns_discovered.name: State Machine Pattern
patterns_discovered.description: Implements state determination logic using when expression to produce SyncState enum variants based on network + pending conditions

patterns_discovered.name: Documentation-First Design
patterns_discovered.description: Extensive KDoc comments explaining state logic, data sources, and state determination rules

gotchas_discovered.issue: Flow.combine emits on any source change
gotchas_discovered.description: Every network state flip or pending count change triggers new emission; could cause excessive UI recompositions without downstream debouncing
No try-catch or error handling for potential OutboxDao or NetworkMonitor failures; errors will crash the flow
SyncState sealed class is referenced but not defined in this diff; assumes it exists with Online, Offline, and Syncing variants
Syncing state always passes progress=null; no actual sync progress tracking implemented

approach_outcome.summary: Successfully created a clean, well-documented repository that combines network connectivity and pending operations into unified sync status
strengths: Clear separation of concerns between network and persistence layers
Concise implementation leveraging Flow API idiomatically

recommendations.recommendation: Add error handling with catchException or similar operators to gracefully handle OutboxDao or NetworkMonitor failures
Consider adding debounceTime() to the combined flow to prevent excessive emissions during rapid network state changes
Implement actual progress tracking instead of hardcoded null; track sync operation count or percentage
Add unit tests for all three state transitions (Offline → Online, Online → Syncing, Syncing → Online) and edge cases
Document SyncState sealed class variants in response if not already done, or cross-reference its documentation
